data.title: Lyft’s London Black Cab Deal And What It Means For Valuation
data.description:
• Lyft (NasdaqGS:LYFT) has agreed to acquire Gett's UK business, a leading London black cab app.
  • The deal is set to roughly double Lyft's London ride volume once integrated.
  • The acquisition expands Lyft's presence in Europe and adds established enterprise relationships in the UK.

Lyft runs a ridehailing platform focused on personal and shared transportation, historically centred on the U.S. and Canada. By moving into London black cabs through Gett's UK business, the company is stepping further into one of Europe's busiest ride markets. For you as an investor, the story now includes regulated black cabs and enterprise clients, not just private hire rides in North America.

This move gives Lyft immediate operational scale in London without building a local network from scratch. The integration and performance of these UK operations, along with any follow on moves in Europe, may become factors to watch alongside earnings, cash flow and regulatory updates in its core markets.

data.title: Rio Tinto Copper Growth And Simandou Milestone Recast Long Term Outlook
data.description:
• LSE:RIO reports a 9% year over year increase in copper equivalent output, led by the ramp up at Oyu Tolgoi.
  • The company completes a land exchange that advances the next stage of the Resolution Copper project.
  • Rio Tinto sends its first high grade iron ore shipment from Simandou to China.
  • Two cyclones affect shipments, yet Pilbara iron ore operations keep production at a high level.

Rio Tinto Group, traded as LSE:RIO, sits at the center of global supply for iron ore, copper and other critical raw materials that feed construction, energy transition and industrial demand. While many recent discussions have focused on share price moves, this latest operational update highlights new copper growth from Oyu Tolgoi, progress at Resolution Copper and first high grade tonnage from Simandou that speak directly to the company’s project pipeline. For investors, these are the kind of real world milestones that help frame where future production may come from.

At the same time, the ability to keep Pilbara iron ore production high through two cyclones points to how the asset base and logistics are handling real disruption. Taken together, these developments provide fresh information to weigh Rio Tinto’s mix of long life growth projects and core iron ore exposure, and to consider how its role in supplying critical minerals could fit within a long term portfolio.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Rio Tinto Group by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Rio Tinto Group.

See which insiders are buying and buying and selling Rio Tinto Group following this latest news.

For shareholders, this update is less about headline production numbers and more about what they signal. Copper output of 229 kt and a 9% rise in copper equivalent volumes, backed by Oyu Tolgoi ramp-up and progress at Resolution Copper and Simandou, point to Rio Tinto steadily leaning into metals tied to electrification and grid investment. At the same time, reaffirmed 2026 guidance across iron ore, copper, aluminium, bauxite and lithium suggests management sees no need to reset expectations following cyclone-related disruption. The first Simandou shipment and continued Pilbara volumes despite two cyclones also speak to operational resilience that income-focused investors often look for in large miners.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Rio Tinto Group Narrative

  • The reported copper growth, lithium output of 12.7 kt LCE and confirmed full year copper guidance support the narrative that new copper and battery-metal projects can contribute to future revenue and margin resilience.
  • The cyclones and ongoing requirement to keep Pilbara production high highlight the operational and cost pressures that the narrative already flags as potential headwinds if conditions or grades become tougher.
  • The first high grade Simandou shipment, and the specific 2026 volume guidance for that asset, are concrete developments that may not yet be fully reflected in earlier versions of the narrative.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Rio Tinto Group to help decide what it is worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Heavy exposure to iron ore means any prolonged period of weaker pricing or higher operating costs at Pilbara could pressure cash flows, especially as capital is committed to copper and lithium projects.
  • ⚠️ Large new projects in Mongolia, Guinea and Latin America carry regulatory, geopolitical and execution risks that could affect timing or economics compared with what current guidance implies.
  • 🎁 The combination of copper production at 229 kt, reaffirmed 2026 guidance of 800 kt to 870 kt and progress at Oyu Tolgoi and Resolution Copper supports the view that Rio Tinto is building a larger position in copper intensive end markets than some peers such as BHP and Glencore.
  • 🎁 Continued strength in Pilbara iron ore volumes, the first Simandou shipment and growing lithium output to 12.7 kt LCE together show a broad resource base that can appeal to investors seeking diversified commodity exposure rather than a single metal story.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, pay close attention to how closely actual 2026 production and sales track the guided ranges, especially for copper, Simandou iron ore and lithium. Any updates on project timelines or costs at Oyu Tolgoi, Resolution Copper and Simandou will help you judge whether current assumptions about growth and risk still hold. It is also worth tracking how quickly cyclone related disruptions are absorbed into shipping performance in later quarters, and how Rio Tinto’s capital allocation and balance sheet develop as spending on growth projects continues.

data.title: Stock Yards Bancorp (SYBT) Earnings Growth And 35.9% Margin Reinforce Bullish Narratives
data.description:
Stock Yards Bancorp (SYBT) has just wrapped up Q4 2025 with total revenue of US$102.7 million and basic EPS of US$1.25, setting the stage for its Q1 2026 reporting season with a trailing twelve month EPS of US$4.77. Over the last six reported quarters, total revenue has moved from US$85.5 million in Q3 2024 to US$102.7 million in Q4 2025, while basic EPS has shifted from US$1.00 to US$1.25. This gives investors a clearer line of sight on how earnings power is tracking into the new year. Net interest margin steady around 3.5%

  • Across the last four quarters, net interest margin sat in a tight band between 3.46% and 3.56%, with the latest trailing twelve month figure at 3.53%.
  • For those who view SYBT as a steady regional franchise, it is notable that this stable margin sits alongside consistent loan balances in the US$6.5 billion to US$7.0 billion range. This suggests the bank has kept pricing and volume relatively balanced, without the swings that more aggressive lenders sometimes experience.

Cost to income ratio just above 53%

The ratio stays in a fairly tight 52.99% to 56.18% range, while trailing net profit margin is 35.9%. This shows that expenses are meaningful but have not prevented SYBT from converting a substantial share of revenue into profit.

Asset quality helped by lower non performing loans

data.title: Has Newsmax (NMAX) Fallen Too Far After A 70% One Year Share Price Slump
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Newsmax at around US$6.71 is a bargain or a value trap, the starting point is understanding what that share price actually builds in.
  • The stock has seen mixed recent returns, with a 5.5% gain over the last 30 days, a 14.8% year to date decline and a steep 70.3% drop over the past year.
  • These moves have kept attention on how investors are reassessing the company, with media coverage focusing on Newsmax's positioning in the U.S. news market and its efforts to grow audience share across cable and digital platforms. That backdrop helps frame the debate about whether recent weakness reflects sentiment, fundamentals, or both.
  • Right now, Newsmax holds a value score of 2 out of 6. The sections that follow will compare different valuation approaches and then finish with a broader way to think about what this score really means for long term investors.

Newsmax scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Newsmax Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company might be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to a present value.

For Newsmax, the latest twelve month free cash flow is a loss of $106.2 million. The model used here is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, which starts with this current position and then applies cash flow projections over the coming years.

The projections used include free cash flow of $19.3 million in 2027 and extend out to 2035, reaching $90.4 million by that year. Only the near term estimate is from an analyst, with the later years extrapolated to build a longer term picture. All figures are in US$.

After discounting these projected cash flows and adding a terminal value, the DCF model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of US$12.86 per share. Compared with the recent share price of about US$6.71, this framework suggests the stock is 47.8% undervalued on these assumptions.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Newsmax is undervalued by 47.8%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 54 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Newsmax.

Approach 2: Newsmax Price vs Sales

For companies where revenue is a key focus, the P/S ratio is often a useful yardstick because it compares what investors are paying for each dollar of sales rather than profit, which can be volatile or negative.

What counts as a “normal” P/S ratio usually reflects how quickly revenue is expected to grow and how risky those cash flows look. Higher expected growth and lower perceived risk can support a higher multiple, while slower growth or higher uncertainty tend to pull it down.

Newsmax currently trades on a P/S ratio of 4.58x. That sits above the Media industry average of 1.16x and also above a peer group average of 0.23x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Newsmax is 1.03x, which is a proprietary estimate of what the P/S might be based on factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market cap and company specific risks.

The Fair Ratio can be more helpful than a simple comparison with peers or the industry because it adjusts for those company specific drivers rather than assuming all media stocks should trade on the same multiple. Set against this Fair Ratio, Newsmax’s current 4.58x P/S suggests the shares are pricing in a materially richer valuation.

Result: OVERVALUED

  • The deal brings Bindplane’s open standards based telemetry pipeline technology into the Dynatrace platform.

For investors watching observability and application performance tools, Dynatrace sits at the intersection of monitoring, security, and AI driven analytics. As software environments grow more complex and data heavy, enterprises are paying closer attention to how telemetry is collected, processed, and stored across clouds and on premises systems.

The planned integration of Bindplane’s technology signals a focus on giving customers more control over what data they ingest, how it is routed, and what it costs. The Bindplane deal points directly at one of the big pressure points in observability, which is the sheer volume and cost of telemetry flowing from cloud and on premises systems. By adding an open standards based pipeline that can sit closer to where data is created, Dynatrace is aiming to let customers filter, shape, and secure data before it hits the core platform. For you as an investor, that sits next to the company’s AI powered Dynatrace Intelligence launch, because both depend on having clean, well managed data feeding the models. It may also speak to customer concerns around compliance and data residency, which are increasingly important as enterprises scale multi cloud observability.

  • Integrating an additional telemetry stack could add execution risk, especially as Dynatrace already faces competition from Datadog, New Relic, and cloud providers that also lean on open standards.
  • The narrative already highlights AI observability and consumption based pricing, but does not fully address how owning an open standards pipeline might affect Dynatrace’s relationship with open source tools such as OpenTelemetry.

data.title: Constellation Energy Reshapes Clean Power Role With Calpine Deal And Crane Test
data.description:
• Constellation Energy has agreed to acquire Calpine in a $26.6b deal, expanding its generation portfolio to around 55 GW and a claimed 10% of U.S. clean energy production.
  • The company is seeking to restart the Crane nuclear plant to support a 20 year clean power supply agreement with Microsoft.
  • Regulatory concerns have emerged, with the PJM Interconnection market monitor questioning the pace and structure of the Crane restart plan.

Constellation Energy, NasdaqGS:CEG, is moving through a period of significant change, with the Calpine acquisition reshaping the business into a much larger and more diversified clean power producer. The company’s current share price stands at $292.77, with a 1 year return of 34.9% and a 3 year gain of about 4x, even as the stock has seen a 20.1% decline year to date. For investors, these mixed share price moves sit alongside a major shift in scale and customer exposure.

The Crane nuclear restart tied to the long term Microsoft agreement adds another layer to consider, because it connects Constellation directly to the growing data center power demand theme while putting regulatory scrutiny in sharper focus. How the company responds to PJM’s concerns and integrates Calpine’s assets could influence its earnings drivers, exposure to power market pricing, and its role in supplying large corporate buyers seeking long duration clean energy contracts.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Constellation Energy by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Constellation Energy.

3 things going right for Constellation Energy that this headline doesn't cover.

The Calpine deal and Crane restart plan together push Constellation further into the center of clean power supply for large corporates, especially data centers. Adding around 55 GW of capacity and a claimed 10% share of U.S. clean energy production gives the company more scale to compete with peers such as NextEra Energy, Vistra and Duke Energy for long-term, contract-based revenue. The 20 year Microsoft agreement effectively pre-sells a portion of future output to a single, creditworthy customer, which can support planning for fuel, maintenance and potential financing. At the same time, the PJM market monitor’s objection to the proposed transfer of Capacity Interconnection Rights highlights how dependent this plan is on regulator acceptance of both timing and structure. For you as an investor, the key tension is clear: larger, contracted exposure to AI-related power demand on one side, and more visible regulatory and execution risk on the other, especially as Constellation integrates Calpine while working through the Crane approvals in a public way.

How This Fits Into The Constellation Energy Narrative

  • PJM’s concerns around the Crane interconnection and capacity rights could challenge assumptions in the narrative about how smoothly nuclear restarts and market access progress.
  • The specific structure of the Microsoft deal and the scale of the Calpine platform are not fully reflected in the existing narrative, which may understate customer concentration and integration complexity.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Constellation Energy to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Regulatory pushback on the Crane restart could delay capacity additions and affect the timing of deliveries under the Microsoft contract.
  • ⚠️ Larger exposure to a few long-duration data center agreements increases customer concentration risk if contract terms or usage patterns change.
  • 🎁 The combined 55 GW portfolio and claimed 10% share of U.S. clean energy output position Constellation to compete for additional long-term corporate and data center deals.
  • 🎁 The Crane restart and Calpine integration support the existing narrative of using nuclear and gas capacity to serve growing AI-driven electricity demand with long-duration contracts.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, watch how regulators rule on Constellation’s requested waivers for Crane, whether any conditions are attached, and how quickly the plant restart schedule evolves. Progress updates on integrating Calpine’s fleet, including any disclosed cost or revenue synergies, will also matter for the earnings mix. On the commercial side, keep an eye on new long-term agreements with data center operators or large corporates, and whether terms look similar to or different from the Microsoft deal. Together, these signposts can help you judge whether Constellation is converting its expanded scale and nuclear footprint into durable, contracted cash flows while keeping regulatory and concentration risks under control.

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business could be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to the present.

For New Found Gold, a 2.4x multiple suggests the market is assigning a lower value to its asset base than peers, even though this is primarily an exploration story with limited revenue and current losses.

Against the Canadian Metals and Mining industry average P/B of 3.3x and a peer group closer to 11.5x, New Found Gold’s current multiple looks restrained. That kind of gap often reflects the company’s lack of meaningful revenue at CA$6m, ongoing losses of CA$47.57m, share dilution over the past year and the early stage nature of the Queensway and Kingsway projects, rather than any clear premium for future cash flows.

data.description:

Valley National Bancorp Q1 2026 earnings snapshot

Valley National Bancorp (VLY) opened 2026 with Q1 results that build on its recent momentum, coming off Q4 2025 total revenue of US$505.9 million and basic EPS of US$0.34, supported by trailing 12 month net income of US$569.0 million and basic EPS of US$1.02. The bank has seen total revenue move from US$365.9 million in Q4 2024 to US$505.9 million in Q4 2025, while quarterly basic EPS stepped up from US$0.20 to US$0.34 over the same period. This sets up Q1 2026 against a backdrop of expanding profitability. With trailing net profit margins and dividends both in focus, investors are likely to read the latest earnings through the lens of how durable these margin levels appear.

See our full analysis for Valley National Bancorp.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to line them up against the most common narratives around Valley National Bancorp to see which stories fit the data and which ones start to look stretched.

See what the community is saying about Valley National Bancorp

Margins and costs point to efficiency gains

  • On a trailing 12 month basis, Valley National Bancorp reported a net profit margin of 30.9%, with a cost to income ratio of 54.44% and net interest margin of 3.05%, compared with a 2.85% net interest margin and 57.98% cost to income ratio for the trailing 12 months a year earlier.
  • Analysts' consensus narrative expects digitalization and operational efficiency to support margins. The recent move from a 2.85% to 3.05% net interest margin and from a 57.98% to 54.44% cost to income ratio provides concrete data for that view, although it also sets a higher bar for maintaining these levels over time.
    • Supporters of the bullish angle on efficiency can point to trailing 12 month net income of US$569.0 million on US$1.8b of revenue as evidence that the current margin structure is already delivering stronger profitability than in the previous year.
    • At the same time, the consensus narrative flags rising compliance and technology costs as an ongoing risk, so any reversal in the cost to income ratio from the current 54.44% level would be important for investors tracking whether the efficiency story is playing out as expected.

Strong recent margin and earnings trends are exactly what bullish investors reference when they talk about Valley's profit story. The dedicated bull case sets out how those themes could play out over time for the business 🐂 Valley National Bancorp Bull Case.

Credit quality: non performing loans creeping higher

  • Non performing loans on a trailing 12 month basis sit at US$433.9 million compared with US$359.5 million a year earlier and US$296.3 million in the period ending Q3 2024, against a loan book that is around US$50.1b.
  • Bears focus on credit risk, especially commercial real estate, and the steady move in non performing loans from US$296.3 million to US$433.9 million gives them specific numbers to point to when questioning how resilient recent earnings and margins might be.
    • Critics highlight that ongoing exposure to commercial real estate and region specific shocks could require higher provisions, and the rise in non performing loans over the last few reported periods is consistent with that concern.
    • On the other hand, the consensus narrative notes that specialized verticals such as healthcare and fund finance have shown low credit losses, so investors may want to separate pockets of stress from areas where asset quality has been described as relatively stable.

For readers who want to see how the cautious credit view stacks up against the numbers sector by sector, the detailed bear case breaks that argument out in full 🐻 Valley National Bancorp Bear Case.

Valuation tension: higher P/E and DCF gap

  • With a current share price of US$13.77, Valley National Bancorp trades on a trailing P/E of 13.4x versus peer and US Banks industry averages of 12.8x and 11.7x respectively, while a DCF fair value of about US$27.50 and an analyst price target of US$14.96 both sit above the current market level.
  • Consensus narrative supporters point to the mix of earnings growth, a 3.2% dividend yield, and the DCF fair value gap as reasons the current price could be out of step with fundamentals, while the higher than peer P/E keeps the discussion grounded in what investors are actually paying today.
    • The trailing 12 month basic EPS of US$1.02 is what underpins both the 13.4x P/E and the DCF fair value of US$27.50, so any shift in earnings would directly affect both sides of that valuation tension.
    • Meanwhile, the analyst price target of US$14.96 is close to the current US$13.77 price, which fits with the idea that, despite a higher P/E than peers, the stock is viewed as roughly fairly valued when forecasts and risks are taken into account.

Approach 1: Axon Enterprise Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model projects the cash Axon Enterprise could generate in the future and then discounts those cash flows back to what they might be worth in today’s dollars.

Axon’s latest twelve month Free Cash Flow is about $92.5 million. Based on analyst inputs and extrapolations, future Free Cash Flow projections reach $455.96 million in 2026 and $966.77 million in 2028, with further estimates extending out to 2035. These figures are expressed in $ and use a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model that blends analyst forecasts with longer term assumptions provided by Simply Wall St.

When all those projected cash flows are discounted to today, the resulting estimated intrinsic value comes out at about $375.52 per share. Compared with the recent share price of around $392.64, the model indicates the stock is about 4.6% overvalued, which is a relatively small gap.

Result: ABOUT RIGHT

Axon Enterprise is fairly valued according to our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF), but this can change at a moment's notice. Track the value in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted on when to act.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Axon Enterprise.

Approach 2: Axon Enterprise Price vs Sales

For companies that are already generating meaningful revenue, the P/S ratio is a useful way to think about what you are paying for each dollar of sales, especially when earnings can be influenced by accounting items or reinvestment decisions.

What counts as a reasonable P/S depends on how quickly investors expect revenue to grow and how risky the business is perceived to be. Higher expected growth and lower perceived risk can justify a higher multiple, while slower growth or higher risk usually call for a lower one.

Axon Enterprise currently trades on a P/S of about 11.38x. That sits above the Aerospace & Defense industry average of roughly 5.02x and also above the peer average of about 7.79x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio framework estimates a P/S of around 17.26x for Axon Enterprise, based on factors such as its growth profile, profit margins, industry, market value and risk characteristics.
